title: Safety profile comparison of chimeric live attenuated and Vero cell-derived inactivated Japanese encephalitis vaccines through an active surveillance system in Australia.
ehr_evidence: Data from SmartVax, an active surveillance system for monitoring vaccine safety Not mentioned None mentioned The study uses a surveillance system (SmartVax) that sends automated SMS messages, rather than an EHR. The study used active vaccine safety surveillance data (SmartVax) rather than EHR. No explicit mention of EHR; data from SmartVax program and clinics. None No mention of EHR or linked health data in the provided references. None
summary: Comparison of safety profiles (AEFIs) between two Japanese encephalitis (JE) vaccines (Imojev and JEspect) in Australia.
key_findings: No significant differences in odds of overall, systemic, or local AEFIs between Imojev and JEspect.
Increased odds of overall AEFI in patients aged <5 years compared to those aged >50 years.
Both vaccines are safe and well tolerated.
No significant difference in AEFI rates between Imojev and JEspect.
Higher odds of overall and local AEFIs in patients aged <5 years compared to those >50 years.
Trend of increased AEFI in females when the number of concomitant vaccines increased.
Both JE vaccines have good post-marketing acute safety profiles.
Imojev presented ~30% higher odds of AEFI compared to JEspect (not statistically significant).
AEFIs are age-dependent and more frequent in children <5 years.
AEFIs were more common in children aged <5 years
No significant increase in AEFI risk in healthy adults receiving concomitant Vero cell-derived inactivated JE and rabies vaccines
Higher risk of AEFIs in females who received $\ge$3 vaccines in addition to JE vaccine
Both Imojev and JEspect vaccines had low rates of AEFIs and were well tolerated.
Higher risk of AEFIs identified in females who received ≥3 vaccines in addition to JE vaccine.
Children aged <5 years should be carefully observed for AEFIs after vaccination.
Both Imojev and JEspect vaccines had low rates of AEFIs.
Imojev is more widely used in Australia due to its one-dose schedule.
Children <5 years should be carefully observed for AEFIs.
Safety and immunogenicity of IXIARO and IMOJEV vaccines were evaluated.
Concomitant administration of JE vaccines with MMR and HAVRIX1440 was studied.
captured_features: AEFI symptoms (tiredness, headache, fever, irritability, sleep disturbance, diarrhea, vomiting, rigors, pain, swelling, rash)
limitations: Data scarce for JE vaccines in non-endemic areas
Small sample size for specific vaccine combinations
Lack of statistical significance in some comparisons
Not statistically significant difference between vaccine types
Sparse evidence on specific vaccine combinations
Small sample size for children <5 years
Severity of symptoms not recorded in the system
SMS follow-up limited to 3-5 days post-immunization
Small sample size for some subgroups (e.g., specific combination of vaccines and by sex)
Need for further studies to verify sex differences and determine if AEFIs are driven by vaccine combination or reactogenicity
